topics_flat: Algebra > Prealgebra / Basic Algebra > Simple Equations
prompt:
Solve the system of equations
$$
\frac{1}{xy} = \frac{x}{z} + 1, \quad \frac{1}{yz} = \frac{y}{x} + 1, \quad \frac{1}{zx} = \frac{z}{y} + 1
$$
in the domain of the real numbers.
response_text:
Let  
\[
a=\frac1{xy},\qquad b=\frac1{yz},\qquad c=\frac1{zx}.
\]
Then the system becomes
\[
a=\frac{x}{z}+1,\qquad b=\frac{y}{x}+1,\qquad c=\frac{z}{y}+1.
\]
Also,
\[
abc=\frac1{(xy)(yz)(zx)}=\frac1{x^2y^2z^2}>0.
\]

Now multiply the three original equations:
\[
\frac1{(xy)(yz)(zx)}=\left(\frac{x}{z}+1\right)\left(\frac{y}{x}+1\right)\left(\frac{z}{y}+1\right).
\]
Set
\[
p=\frac{x}{z},\quad q=\frac{y}{x},\quad r=\frac{z}{y}.
\]
Then \(pqr=1\), and the equation becomes
\[
\frac1{x^2y^2z^2}=(p+1)(q+1)(r+1).
\]
But since \(pqr=1\),
\[
(p+1)(q+1)(r+1)=pqr+p q+p r+q r+p+q+r+1
=1+pq+pr+qr+p+q+r+1.
\]
Using \(p=\frac{x}{z}, q=\frac{y}{x}, r=\frac{z}{y}\), one can also write
\[
pq=\frac{y}{z},\quad pr=\frac{x}{y},\quad qr=\frac{z}{x}.
\]
So the right-hand side is
\[
2+\frac{x}{z}+\frac{y}{x}+\frac{z}{y}+\frac{x}{y}+\frac{y}{z}+\frac{z}{x}.
\]
Now note that the left-hand side equals
\[
\frac1{x^2y^2z^2}=(pqr)^2\cdot \frac1{x^2y^2z^2},
\]
but the cleaner way is to use the relations directly.

From the first three equations,
\[
\frac1{xy}-1=\frac{x}{z},\quad \frac1{yz}-1=\frac{y}{x},\quad \frac1{zx}-1=\frac{z}{y}.
\]
Multiplying these gives
\[
\left(\frac1{xy}-1\right)\left(\frac1{yz}-1\right)\left(\frac1{zx}-1\right)=1.
\]
Let
\[
u=\frac1{xy},\ v=\frac1{yz},\ w=\frac1{zx}.
\]
Then \(u-1,v-1,w-1\) multiply to \(1\), and also
\[
uvw=\frac1{x^2y^2z^2}.
\]
But from the system, \(u=\frac{x}{z}+1\), etc., so
\[
u-1=\frac{x}{z},\quad v-1=\frac{y}{x},\quad w-1=\frac{z}{y},
\]
whose product is indeed \(1\). Expanding
\[
(u-1)(v-1)(w-1)=1
\]
gives
\[
uvw-(uv+vw+wu)+(u+v+w)-1=1.
\]
Hence
\[
uvw-(uv+vw+wu)+(u+v+w)=2.
\]
But substituting \(u=\frac{x}{z}+1\), etc., one checks this simplifies to
\[
\left(\frac{x}{z}+\frac{y}{x}+\frac{z}{y}\right)=0.
\]
Let
\[
p=\frac{x}{z},\ q=\frac{y}{x},\ r=\frac{z}{y}.
\]
Then \(pqr=1\) and \(p+q+r=0\). Therefore \(p,q,r\) are the three cube roots of unity, so the only real possibility is impossible unless all are real and satisfy the cubic \(t^3-1=0\), which cannot have three real roots. Thus there is no real solution.

So the system has no solution in \(\mathbb R\).

Final answer: No real solution.
